Business justification: Clinicians must validate against national NPI registry for CMS enrollment, payer credentialing, and primary source verification. Healthcare operations perform monthly NPI validation checks, deactivati']
  board_certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the clinician\'s primary ABMS board certification. Nullable for clinicians with lifetime certification or non-physician providers. Used to trigger recertification reminders in Symplr.']
  board_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician holds active board certification from an ABMS-recognized specialty board in their primary specialty. True = currently board certified; False = not board certified or certification lapsed. Used in credentialing, network adequacy, and quality reporting.']
  caqh_provider_number varchar [note: 'Unique 8-digit identifier assigned to the clinician in the CAQH ProView universal credentialing database. Used to streamline payer credentialing and enrollment by providing a single repository of provider credentials. Sourced from Symplr and CAQH ProView.']
  clinician_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the clinician\'s professional category. Drives role-based access, workflow routing, and analytics segmentation. Physician includes MD/DO; advanced_practice includes NP/PA; nursing includes RN/LPN; allied_health includes PT/OT/RT; pharmacy includes PharmD/RPh; behavioral_health includes LCSW/psychologist.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clinician record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Represents the record audit creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and HIPAA audit log requirements.']
  credentialing_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinician\'s current credentialing approval expires and re-credentialing must be completed. Typically a 2-year cycle per Joint Commission standards. Used to trigger re-credentialing workflows in Symplr.']
  credentialing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the clinician\'s credentialing and privileging review within the organization. Credentialed indicates approved to practice; pending indicates initial or re-credentialing in progress; expired indicates lapsed credentials requiring renewal. Sourced from Symplr credentialing system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Clinician\'s date of birth. Used for identity verification during credentialing, primary source verification, and DEA registration. PHI/PII — access restricted per HIPAA.']
  dea_number varchar [note: 'DEA registration number authorizing the clinician to prescribe controlled substances. Format is two letters followed by seven digits. Required for prescribing Schedule II–V medications. Sourced from Symplr credentialing and Epic Willow pharmacy module.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment lifecycle status of the clinician within the organization. Active indicates currently practicing; on_leave includes FMLA and medical leave; suspended indicates temporary practice restriction; terminated indicates separation. Sourced from Workday HCM.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the clinician\'s employment relationship with the healthcare organization. Drives payroll processing, malpractice coverage determination, and credentialing workflows. Sourced from Workday HCM Core HR.']
  fellowship_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinician completed their fellowship training program. Nullable for clinicians without fellowship training. Used for subspecialty credentialing and privileging decisions. Sourced from Symplr.']
  fellowship_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the fellowship training program completed by the clinician (e.g., Interventional Cardiology Fellowship at Cleveland Clinic). Part of the embedded postgraduate training history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Nullable for clinicians without fellowship training. Sourced from Symplr.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first (given) name of the clinician as recorded in the provider master. Used for identity verification, credentialing, and provider directory display. Sourced from Epic EHR provider master and Symplr.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Clinician\'s self-reported gender identity. Used for workforce diversity reporting, EEOC compliance, and provider directory display where applicable. Restricted per HIPAA and applicable employment privacy regulations.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinician was officially hired or onboarded by the healthcare organization. Used for tenure calculations, benefits eligibility, and workforce analytics. Sourced from Workday HCM Core HR.']
  internship_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinician completed their internship or PGY-1 training. Nullable where internship is embedded within residency program. Sourced from Symplr primary source verification.']
  internship_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internship or postgraduate year 1 (PGY-1) program completed by the clinician. Part of the embedded postgraduate training history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Nullable for clinicians where internship is embedded within residency. Sourced from Symplr.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last (family) name of the clinician as recorded in the provider master. Used for identity verification, credentialing, provider directory, and billing. Sourced from Epic EHR provider master and Symplr.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the clinician\'s primary state medical or professional license. Used by credentialing teams to trigger renewal workflows and ensure continuous practice authorization. Sourced from Symplr credentialing.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation for the jurisdiction that issued the primary state medical or professional license. Used for practice authorization validation and regulatory reporting.']
  malpractice_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the clinician\'s professional liability (malpractice) insurance policy. Used to ensure continuous coverage and trigger renewal alerts in Symplr credentialing workflows.']
  malpractice_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the clinician\'s professional liability (malpractice) insurance coverage. Required for credentialing and hospital privileging. Sourced from Symplr credentialing. Confidential business data.']
  medicaid_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician is actively enrolled as a Medicaid provider in their primary state. True = enrolled and active; False = not enrolled. Critical for revenue cycle management and claims submission for Medicaid patients.']
  medical_degree varchar [note: 'Highest professional degree awarded to the clinician upon completion of medical or professional school (e.g., MD, DO, MBBS, PharmD, DNP). Used in credentialing, provider directory, and billing. Sourced from Symplr primary source verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|MBBS|PharmD|PhD|DNP|MSN|PA|NP|DDS|DPM — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  medical_school_graduation_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinician graduated from medical or professional school and received their degree. Used for credentialing, primary source verification, and training history completeness. Sourced from Symplr.']
  medical_school_name varchar [note: 'Name of the institution from which the clinician received their medical or professional degree (e.g., Johns Hopkins School of Medicine). Part of the embedded education history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Sourced from Symplr credentialing primary source verification.']
  medicare_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician is actively enrolled as a Medicare provider with CMS. True = enrolled and active; False = not enrolled or opted out. Critical for revenue cycle management and claims submission. Sourced from CMS PECOS (Provider Enrollment, Chain, and Ownership System).']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Legal middle name or initial of the clinician. Used for identity disambiguation when first and last names are not unique. Sourced from Epic EHR provider master.']
  name_suffix varchar [note: 'Generational or professional suffix appended to the clinician\'s name (e.g., Jr., Sr., II, III). Distinct from professional credentials/degrees which are stored separately.']
  oig_exclusion_check_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the clinician was screened against the OIG List of Excluded Individuals and Entities (LEIE). CMS requires monthly screening for all providers billing federal healthcare programs.']
  oig_exclusion_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician has been screened against the OIG List of Excluded Individuals and Entities (LEIE). Required monthly per CMS guidance to prevent billing for services rendered by excluded providers. True = checked and clear; False = not yet checked or excluded.']
  payer_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate enrollment status of the clinician with payers for billing purposes. Enrolled indicates the clinician is credentialed and enrolled with at least one active payer; pending indicates enrollment applications in progress. Detailed per-payer enrollment is tracked in the provider_payer_enrollment product. Sourced from Symplr provider enrollment module.']
  primary_source_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician\'s credentials (education, training, licensure) have been verified directly with the issuing source (primary source verification) as required by The Joint Commission and NCQA credentialing standards. True = verified; False = not yet verified or verification pending.']
  professional_designation varchar [note: 'Highest or primary professional credential/degree designation displayed after the clinician\'s name (e.g., MD, DO, NP, PA-C, RN, PharmD, PhD). Used in provider directories, clinical documentation headers, and credentialing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|NP|PA-C|RN|PharmD|PhD|DDS|DPM|OD|CRNA|CNM|LCSW|PT|OT — promote to reference product]']
  residency_acgme_accredited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician\'s residency program was accredited by the Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education (ACGME) at the time of completion. True = ACGME accredited; False = not accredited or international program. Required for privileging decisions.']
  residency_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinician completed their postgraduate residency training program. Used for credentialing and training history verification. Nullable for clinicians who did not complete a residency (e.g., some allied health professionals). Sourced from Symplr.']
  residency_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ACGME-accredited residency program completed by the clinician (e.g., Internal Medicine Residency at Massachusetts General Hospital). Part of the embedded postgraduate training history per the clinician product SSOT definition. Sourced from Symplr.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'Primary state medical or professional license number issued by the applicable state licensing board. Represents the clinician\'s authorization to practice in their primary state of licensure. Additional multi-state licenses are tracked in the clinician_license product.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinician\'s employment or engagement with the organization ended. Nullable for active clinicians. Used to deactivate system access, revoke privileges, and trigger payer disenrollment workflows. Sourced from Workday HCM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clinician record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance. Updated on every write operation.']
  work_email varchar [note: 'Clinician\'s primary organizational work email address. Used for secure communications, system access provisioning, credentialing notifications, and provider directory. Sourced from Workday HCM and Epic provider master.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'Clinician\'s primary work phone number including direct line or pager number. Used for clinical communications, on-call scheduling, and credentialing contact. Sourced from Epic provider master and Workday HCM.']

  Note: 'Master record for all individual healthcare professionals including physicians, nurses, allied health professionals, and advanced practice providers. Serves as the SSOT for provider identity, NPI (National Provider Identifier), DEA number, professional demographics, primary specialty, and employment type. Includes complete educational background and postgraduate training history as embedded detail records: medical school, residency programs, fellowship training, and internships with institution name, program type, degree or certificate awarded, graduation/completion date, ACGME accreditation status, and primary source verification status. No other product in this domain stores education or training history. Sourced from Epic EHR provider master and Symplr credentialing system.'
}

Table provider.org_provider {
  org_provider_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the organizational provider record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the org_provider data product.']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.organization. Business justification: Organizational hierarchy management: an org_provider (hospital, clinic) belongs to a parent health system (organization). Required for network management, payer contracting, and CMS organizational rep']
  reference_npi_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.npi_registry. Business justification: Organizations validate against NPI registry for CMS certification, PECOS enrollment, and organizational credentialing. Healthcare operations require this for Type 2 NPI validation, authorized official']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The accrediting organization that granted the current accreditation status (e.g., The Joint Commission (TJC), DNV GL Healthcare, Healthcare Facilities Accreditation Program (HFAP), Accreditation Association for Ambulatory Health Care (AAAHC)). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TJC|DNV_GL|HFAP|AAAHC|ACHC|CARF|URAC|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the current accreditation certificate expires. Used to trigger renewal workflows and monitor compliance with accreditation maintenance requirements.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'The primary street address of the organizational provider\'s physical service location. Used for claims billing, provider directory, and regulatory correspondence.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, building) for the organizational provider\'s physical service location.']
  bed_count varchar [note: 'The total number of licensed inpatient beds at the facility as reported to the state Department of Health and CMS. Used for capacity planning, DRG-based reimbursement analysis, and regulatory reporting. Applicable to inpatient facilities; null for outpatient-only organizations.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the organizational provider\'s primary service location. Used for geographic network analysis, provider directory, and regulatory reporting.']
  cms_certification_number varchar [note: 'The 6-digit CMS Certification Number (formerly Medicare Provider Number) assigned to certified healthcare facilities by CMS. Required for Medicare and Medicaid participation and cost reporting.']
  county varchar [note: 'County of the organizational provider\'s primary service location. Used for population health management, HRSA Health Professional Shortage Area (HPSA) designation, and rural health program eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the organizational provider record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and HIPAA audit log requirements.']
  credentialing_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the organizational provider\'s current credentialing cycle expires. Triggers re-credentialing workflows in Symplr to maintain uninterrupted network participation and claims payment eligibility.']
  credentialing_status varchar [note: 'Current credentialing status of the organizational provider as maintained in the credentialing system (Symplr). Tracks whether the organization has completed payer and network credentialing requirements for participation.']
  critical_access_hospital_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds Critical Access Hospital (CAH) designation from CMS, qualifying it for cost-based Medicare reimbursement. CAH designation requires rural location, 25 or fewer acute care beds, and 24/7 emergency services.']
  disproportionate_share_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility qualifies for CMS Disproportionate Share Hospital (DSH) payment adjustments, which compensate hospitals serving a high proportion of low-income and Medicaid patients.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'The trade or operating name under which the organization conducts business if different from the legal name. Commonly used in patient-facing communications and signage.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the organizational provider record became effective and the organization was authorized to participate in applicable programs and networks. Used for enrollment tracking and retroactive claims adjudication.']
  ehr_system varchar [note: 'The primary Electronic Health Record (EHR) or Electronic Medical Record (EMR) system deployed at the organizational provider. Used for HIE connectivity planning, HL7/FHIR interface configuration, and interoperability assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: epic|cerner|meditech|allscripts|athenahealth|other|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current CMS enrollment status of the organizational provider in Medicare and/or Medicaid programs. Distinct from general provider_status; specifically tracks payer program participation eligibility.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational provider by facility type (e.g., acute care hospital, critical access hospital, ambulatory surgery center, federally qualified health center, skilled nursing facility, outpatient clinic, group practice). Drives billing rules, regulatory requirements, and reimbursement methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acute_care_hospital|critical_access_hospital|ambulatory_surgery_center|federally_qualified_health_center|skilled_nursing_facility|outpatient_clinic|group_practice|long_term_care|behavioral_health|home_health|hospice|urgent_care — promote to reference product]']
  fax varchar [note: 'Primary fax number for the organizational provider. Used for clinical document transmission, referral communications, and payer correspondence in compliance with HIPAA secure transmission requirements.']
  fhir_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'The base URL of the organizational provider\'s HL7 FHIR R4 API endpoint for electronic health information exchange. Required under the CMS Interoperability and Patient Access Rule for payer-to-provider data exchange.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the organizational provider as registered with state licensing authorities, CMS, and the IRS. Used for billing, contracting, and regulatory submissions.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code where the facility operating license was issued. Determines applicable state regulatory requirements and Medicaid program participation rules.']
  medicaid_provider_number varchar [note: 'State-assigned Medicaid provider identification number for the organizational provider. Required for Medicaid claims submission and program participation. Format varies by state Medicaid Management Information System (MMIS).']
  medicare_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organizational provider has an active Medicare participation agreement with CMS, authorizing it to bill Medicare for covered services at the Medicare-approved rate.']
  network_participation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the organizational provider\'s participation in contracted payer networks. Drives claims adjudication, patient cost-sharing calculations, and network adequacy reporting to state and federal regulators.']
  oig_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organizational provider is currently listed on the OIG List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE). Excluded providers are prohibited from participating in federal healthcare programs. Must be checked prior to contracting and claims payment.']
  organization_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the organizational provider\'s structural type (e.g., hospital, clinic, group practice, Accountable Care Organization (ACO), Integrated Delivery Organization (IDO), health system, outpatient center). Distinct from facility_type which captures the CMS/regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hospital|clinic|group_practice|aco|ido|health_system|outpatient_center — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'The ownership classification of the organizational provider indicating whether it is non-profit, for-profit, government-owned, tribal, or faith-based. Affects tax status, CMS cost reporting requirements, and regulatory obligations.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the organizational provider\'s main administrative or clinical contact line. Used for referral coordination, payer communications, and provider directory.']
  primary_specialty varchar [note: 'The primary clinical specialty or service line of the organizational provider as designated in NPPES and CMS enrollment records (e.g., General Acute Care Hospital, Multispecialty Group Practice, Radiology Center). Used for network directory, referral routing, and quality reporting.']
  provider_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational provider record. Drives eligibility for claims submission, referral routing, and network directory inclusion. \'Active\' indicates the organization is currently enrolled and operational; \'pending\' indicates enrollment or credentialing in progress.']
  sam_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organizational provider is listed in the federal SAM.gov exclusions database, which prohibits participation in federal contracts and assistance programs including CMS programs.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this organizational provider record was sourced (e.g., Epic, Cerner Millennium, Symplr, NPPES, CMS PECOS). Used for data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: epic|cerner|meditech|symplr|nppes|pecos|manual|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the organizational provider\'s primary service location. Determines applicable state regulations, Medicaid program rules, and network adequacy requirements.']
  state_license_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the state-issued facility operating license. Triggers license renewal workflows and compliance monitoring to prevent lapse in operating authority.']
  state_license_number varchar [note: 'The facility license number issued by the state Department of Health authorizing the organization to operate as a healthcare facility. Required for state regulatory compliance and CMS certification.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'The federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax Identification Number (TIN) assigned by the IRS to the organizational provider. Required for claims billing, 1099 reporting, and payer contracting.']
  teaching_status varchar [note: 'Designation of the facility\'s teaching status as recognized by CMS for Indirect Medical Education (IME) and Direct Graduate Medical Education (DGME) payment adjustments. Major teaching hospitals have a resident-to-bed ratio above CMS thresholds.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the organizational provider\'s participation or enrollment was terminated. Null for active providers. Used for claims adjudication, network directory management, and regulatory reporting.']
  trauma_level varchar [note: 'The American College of Surgeons (ACS) or state-designated trauma center level (I through V) indicating the facility\'s trauma care capability. Level I provides the highest level of comprehensive trauma care. Used for ED routing, transfer protocols, and quality reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the organizational provider record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), ETL incremental loads, and audit trail compliance.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'The public-facing website URL of the organizational provider. Used in provider directory, patient portal, and Salesforce Health Cloud patient relationship management.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the organizational provider\'s primary service location. Used for geographic network analysis, population health mapping, and claims processing.']

  Note: 'Master record for organizational providers including hospitals, clinics, group practices, outpatient facilities, and integrated delivery networks. Captures organizational NPI (Type 2), tax identification number, CMS certification number, facility type, accreditation status, and organizational hierarchy. Distinct from individual clinician records.'
}

Table provider.specialty {
  specialty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each specialty record in the provider specialty reference catalog. Primary key for the specialty data product. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a reference/lookup catalog of recognized medical specialties and NUCC taxonomy classifications; per-role minimums for REFERENCE_LOOKUP are exempt.]']
  primary_cpt_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Specialties define authorized procedure sets for privileging decisions, prior authorization rules, and payer contracting. Real healthcare operations require this link for automated privilege delineati']
  primary_icd_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Specialties have primary diagnosis domains for referral routing, utilization management, and network adequacy calculations. Healthcare operations use this for automated referral validation, specialty-']
  snomed_concept_id varchar [note: 'The SNOMED CT concept identifier corresponding to this specialty for clinical terminology interoperability and HL7 FHIR resource alignment. Supports HIE data exchange and FHIR PractitionerRole resource population.']
  abms_board_name varchar [note: 'The specific American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS) member board responsible for certifying physicians in this specialty (e.g., \'American Board of Internal Medicine\', \'American Board of Pediatrics\'). Used in credentialing verification and Symplr primary source verification workflows.']
  acgme_program_code varchar [note: 'The ACGME program specialty code used to identify accredited residency and fellowship training programs in this specialty. Used for graduate medical education (GME) tracking, resident credentialing, and workforce planning in Workday HCM.']
  board_certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the recognized medical board or certifying organization that issues board certification for this specialty (e.g., \'American Board of Internal Medicine (ABIM)\', \'American Board of Surgery (ABS)\', \'American Board of Psychiatry and Neurology (ABPN)\'). Used in credentialing workflows and provider directory publishing.']
  board_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether board certification is required for a provider to be credentialed and privileged in this specialty at the organization. When true, credentialing workflows in Symplr enforce board certification verification before granting privileges.']
  cms_enrollment_specialty_type varchar [note: 'The CMS-defined specialty type label used on provider enrollment applications (CMS-855 series forms) for Medicare and Medicaid enrollment. Distinct from the two-digit CMS specialty code; represents the descriptive label used in enrollment workflows.']
  cms_specialty_code varchar [note: 'The two-digit CMS specialty code used in Medicare and Medicaid claims processing and provider enrollment. Derived from the CMS crosswalk to NUCC taxonomy codes. Required for CMS-1500 and UB-04 claim form submission and CMS provider enrollment taxonomy reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this specialty record was first created in the enterprise reference catalog. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  dea_registration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in this specialty are required to hold an active DEA registration number for controlled substance prescribing authority. Relevant for specialties such as pain management, psychiatry, and anesthesiology. Used in payer enrollment and credentialing validation.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence controlling the display order of specialties in provider directory user interfaces, credentialing dropdown menus, and referral routing selection screens. Lower values appear first in sorted lists.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this specialty code and its associated NUCC taxonomy classification became effective and valid for use in provider credentialing, enrollment, and claims submission. Supports temporal validity tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which this specialty code and taxonomy classification was retired, superseded, or became invalid. Null indicates the specialty is currently active. Used to enforce temporal validity in claims submission and provider enrollment workflows.']
  fhir_practitioner_role_code varchar [note: 'The HL7 FHIR R4 PractitionerRole.code value corresponding to this specialty for FHIR-compliant API responses and HIE data exchange. Enables alignment with FHIR-based provider directory publishing and ONC interoperability requirements.']
  hedis_measure_set varchar [note: 'Identifies the applicable HEDIS measure set(s) associated with this specialty for quality measurement and NCQA reporting purposes (e.g., \'Preventive Care\', \'Chronic Conditions\', \'Behavioral Health\'). Used in Epic Healthy Planet and population health analytics.']
  hospital_privileges_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in this specialty are subject to hospital privileging and credentialing requirements for inpatient or procedural care delivery. Drives privileging workflow routing in Symplr and The Joint Commission compliance tracking.']
  mips_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in this specialty are subject to Merit-based Incentive Payment System (MIPS) reporting requirements under MACRA. Used for quality reporting program enrollment and CMS value-based payment calculations.']
  network_adequacy_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the specialty for CMS and state network adequacy standards, determining minimum provider-to-enrollee ratios and geographic access requirements. Used in network management, payer contracting, and CMS network adequacy reporting.']
  npi_taxonomy_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specialty/taxonomy code is eligible to be declared as a taxonomy code on an NPI application or update with the National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES). Ensures only valid NUCC taxonomy codes are submitted during NPI registry alignment.']
  nucc_classification varchar [note: 'The second level of the NUCC taxonomy hierarchy representing the provider classification within the provider type (e.g., \'Family Medicine\', \'Internal Medicine\', \'Psychiatry & Neurology\'). Used for payer enrollment specialty validation and network management.']
  nucc_provider_type varchar [note: 'The broadest level of the NUCC taxonomy hierarchy representing the provider type category (e.g., \'Allopathic & Osteopathic Physicians\', \'Nursing Service Providers\', \'Behavioral Health & Social Service Providers\'). First level of the three-level NUCC classification hierarchy.']
  nucc_specialization varchar [note: 'The third and most granular level of the NUCC taxonomy hierarchy representing the specific area of specialization within the classification (e.g., \'Adolescent Medicine\', \'Geriatric Medicine\', \'Sports Medicine\'). May be null for specialties without a defined subspecialization.']
  nucc_taxonomy_code varchar [note: 'The 10-character alphanumeric NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy code that uniquely classifies the provider type, classification, and specialization. Required for NPI registry alignment, claims submission, and CMS provider enrollment taxonomy reporting. This product is the SSOT for all NUCC taxonomy codes in the provider domain.']
  payer_enrollment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specialty is eligible for payer enrollment and credentialing with commercial and government payers. Specialties marked false may be internal classifications not recognized by external payers for enrollment purposes.']
  pecos_specialty_code varchar [note: 'The specialty code used within the CMS Provider Enrollment Chain and Ownership System (PECOS) for Medicare provider enrollment and revalidation. Required for CMS enrollment submissions and RAC audit compliance.']
  prescribing_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in this specialty hold prescribing authority for medications under applicable state and federal law. Used in Epic Willow pharmacy integration, MAR workflows, and CPOE order routing to validate prescriber eligibility.']
  primary_care_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specialty qualifies for Primary Care Physician (PCP) designation in managed care networks including HMO, ACO, and PCMH models. Drives PCP assignment logic in Salesforce Health Cloud and Epic Healthy Planet population health workflows.']
  prior_authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether services rendered by providers in this specialty typically require prior authorization from payers before service delivery. Used in Epic order management (CPOE) and revenue cycle pre-authorization workflows.']
  referral_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal referral is required to access providers in this specialty under standard managed care plan rules (HMO, POS). Drives referral authorization workflows in Epic Cadence and Salesforce Health Cloud referral management.']
  rvu_work_component decimal(10,4) [note: 'The standard physician work Relative Value Unit (RVU) component associated with the specialty, used as a baseline for productivity benchmarking and compensation modeling. Sourced from CMS Physician Fee Schedule. Used in Workday HCM compensation planning and RCM analytics.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Abbreviated description of the specialty suitable for display in constrained UI contexts such as mobile provider directories, claim form dropdowns, and scheduling system specialty selectors. Maximum 100 characters.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the authoritative source system from which this specialty record was ingested or maintained (e.g., NUCC taxonomy feed, CMS specialty crosswalk, Epic EHR, Symplr credentialing). Used for data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Silver layer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nucc|cms|epic|cerner|symplr|meditech|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  specialty_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping category for the specialty used for network management, referral routing, and provider directory segmentation. Classifies specialties into broad functional areas for operational and analytical purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|surgical|behavioral_health|allied_health|nursing|dental|pharmacy|ancillary|administrative|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  specialty_code varchar [note: 'Internally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the specialty within the enterprise provider directory and credentialing systems. Used as the business key for specialty lookups across Epic, Cerner, and Symplr integrations.']
  specialty_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specialty including scope of practice, patient population served, and types of conditions or procedures managed. Used in provider directory publishing, patient-facing portals, and referral routing guidance.']
  specialty_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the medical or clinical specialty (e.g., \'Internal Medicine\', \'Orthopedic Surgery\', \'Pediatric Cardiology\'). Used in provider directories, credentialing workflows, and payer enrollment forms.']
  specialty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specialty record in the enterprise reference catalog. Active records are available for provider credentialing, enrollment, and claims submission. Deprecated records are retained for historical reference only.']
  specialty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specialty as primary care, specialist, subspecialist, hospitalist, advanced practice, or ancillary. Used for referral routing logic, network adequacy analysis, and PCP designation in HMO and ACO network management.']
  surgical_specialty boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specialty is classified as a surgical specialty, requiring operating room (OR) scheduling, surgical credentialing, and OpTime/SurgiNet integration for case scheduling and documentation.']
  telehealth_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether providers in this specialty are eligible to deliver services via telehealth modalities under applicable CMS and state regulations. Used for network directory publishing, referral routing, and telehealth program management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this specialty record was last modified in the enterprise reference catalog. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The NUCC Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set version number in which this specialty code was introduced or last updated (e.g., \'23.1\', \'22.0\'). Enables version-specific taxonomy lookups for historical claims reprocessing and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Unified reference catalog of recognized medical and clinical specialties, subspecialties, and NUCC (National Uniform Claim Committee) Health Care Provider Taxonomy classifications. Includes specialty code, NUCC taxonomy code, provider type, classification, specialization, board certification body, specialty category, CMS crosswalk to specialty codes, effective date, and whether the specialty requires additional credentialing. Serves as the single consolidated SSOT for all provider classification, taxonomy, and specialization data — no other product in this domain stores NUCC taxonomy codes, provider taxonomy classifications, or specialty definitions. Used for provider directory publishing, network management, referral routing, NPI registry alignment, claims submission specialty coding, payer enrollment specialty validation, and CMS provider enrollment taxonomy reporting.'
}

Table provider.credential {
  credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each credential record in the provider credentialing master. Primary key for the credential data product.']
  authorized_cpt_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Procedure-specific credentials (surgical certifications, interventional licenses) authorize performance of specific CPT codes. Healthcare operations use this for privilege delineation, peer review cas']
  clinician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual clinician or healthcare professional who holds this credential. Links to the provider master record.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: DEA credentials authorize controlled substance prescribing by schedule (II-V). Material_master tracks controlled substances with dea_schedule. Link enables regulatory compliance verification that clin']
  specialty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Credentials are specialty-specific (e.g., board certifications, specialty licenses). credential.specialty_name should be normalized to specialty table. FK will be populated at credential creation.']
  training_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training. Business justification: Credentials like DEA X-waiver require documented training completion. Credentialing systems verify training prerequisites before credential issuance/renewal per regulatory requirements (e.g., 8-hour b']
  board_action_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent disciplinary action or adverse action was taken by the issuing authority against this credential. Null if no board action exists. Used in conjunction with board_action_flag for credentialing compliance reporting.']
  board_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a disciplinary action, sanction, reprimand, or adverse action has been taken by the issuing authority against this credential. True = board action exists; False = no board action. Triggers mandatory review in credentialing and privileging workflows.']
  caqh_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential has been submitted to the CAQH ProView universal credentialing database. CAQH ProView is the industry-standard repository used by most commercial payers for provider credentialing and enrollment verification.']
  certifying_board_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the specialty board that issued the board certification (e.g., \'American Board of Internal Medicine\', \'American Board of Surgery\', \'American Osteopathic Board of Internal Medicine\'). Applicable only to board certification credential types.']
  cme_accrediting_organization varchar [note: 'The organization that accredited the CME activity (e.g., \'AMA PRA\', \'ACCME\', \'ANCC\', \'ACPE\'). Determines whether the CME credits are recognized for state licensure renewal and board recertification purposes.']
  cme_activity_title varchar [note: 'The official title of the CME (Continuing Medical Education) activity as designated by the accrediting organization. Applicable only to CME activity credential types. Used for CME compliance tracking and licensure renewal documentation.']
  cme_activity_type varchar [note: 'The format or delivery modality of the CME activity as classified by the accrediting organization (e.g., live conference, enduring material, performance improvement activity). Applicable only to CME activity credential types.']
  cme_category varchar [note: 'The AMA PRA CME category designation for this activity. Category 1 credits are formally accredited and required for most state licensure renewals; Category 2 credits are self-designated and have more limited acceptance.']
  cme_credit_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of CME credit hours awarded upon completion of this CME activity. Used to track cumulative CME hours for state licensure renewal requirements and board recertification MOC compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail requirements under HIPAA, HITRUST, and ISO 27001 for credentialing data governance.']
  credential_number varchar [note: 'The externally-issued identifier for this credential as assigned by the issuing authority (e.g., state license number, DEA registration number, board certification number, CME activity ID). This is the official reference number used in regulatory and payer verification workflows.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credential as reported by the issuing authority or verified through primary source verification. Active credentials authorize clinical practice; expired or revoked credentials trigger immediate workflow alerts in credentialing and pharmacy order validation systems.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the credential record. Distinguishes among state medical/nursing licenses, DEA controlled substance registrations, specialty board certifications, and CME (Continuing Medical Education) activity completions. Drives downstream workflow routing in credentialing and privileging systems such as Symplr.']
  days_to_expiration varchar [note: 'The number of days remaining until this credential expires, calculated from the current date to the expiration_date. Used for proactive renewal alerting and credentialing dashboard KPIs. Negative values indicate the credential is already expired.']
  dea_business_activity_type varchar [note: 'The DEA-designated business activity type associated with the DEA registration. Defines the authorized scope of controlled substance handling (e.g., practitioner prescribing vs. hospital dispensing vs. research). Applicable only to DEA registration credential types.']
  dea_schedule_authorizations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of controlled substance schedule authorizations granted under this DEA registration (e.g., \'II,III,IV,V\'). Applicable only to DEA registration credential types. Used by pharmacy order validation systems (Epic Willow, Cerner PharmNet) to authorize controlled substance prescribing.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this credential record is considered effective and authorizes clinical practice or activity. For state licenses and DEA registrations, this aligns with the issue_date. For board certifications, this is the initial certification effective date. Supports temporal validity queries.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this credential record is considered effective. Aligns with expiration_date for most credential types. Null for open-ended or lifetime credentials. Supports SCD (Slowly Changing Dimension) and temporal validity queries in the lakehouse.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the credential expires and must be renewed or recertified to remain valid. Null for credentials with no expiration (e.g., certain lifetime board certifications). Drives automated renewal alerts in Symplr and Epic credentialing workflows.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the credential was originally issued or first conferred by the issuing authority. For board certifications, this is the initial certification date. For CME activities, this is the activity completion date.']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the organization or governmental body that issued or confers this credential (e.g., \'Texas Medical Board\', \'U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration\', \'American Board of Internal Medicine\', \'American Medical Association — PRA\'). Used for primary source verification routing.']
  issuing_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or territory abbreviation where the credential was issued. Applicable to state medical licenses and DEA state-specific registrations. Null for federal DEA registrations, board certifications, and CME activities that are not state-specific.']
  moc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the provider\'s compliance with the Maintenance of Certification (MOC) program requirements for this board certification. MOC compliance is required by many hospitals for privileging and by payers for network participation. Applicable only to board certification credential types.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for credentialing staff to document relevant context, exceptions, or special circumstances associated with this credential record (e.g., pending renewal documentation, board action history, conditional license restrictions). Not used for structured data.']
  npdb_queried boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) was queried as part of the credentialing verification process for this provider. NPDB queries are required by CMS and The Joint Commission for initial credentialing and re-credentialing.']
  npdb_query_date date [note: 'The date on which the NPDB was queried for this provider\'s credentialing record. Required documentation for CMS and Joint Commission credentialing compliance audits.']
  oig_exclusion_check_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the OIG LEIE (List of Excluded Individuals/Entities) was checked for this provider. CMS recommends monthly screening; documents compliance for RAC audits and OIG investigations.']
  oig_exclusion_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OIG (Office of Inspector General) List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE) was checked for this provider as part of credentialing. OIG exclusion screening is required by CMS; employing an excluded provider can result in significant civil monetary penalties.']
  payer_enrollment_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is required for or submitted as part of payer enrollment and network participation applications. True = credential is submitted to payers (e.g., state license, DEA, board certification); False = not required for payer enrollment.']
  primary_source_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential has been verified directly with the issuing authority (primary source verification). True = verified with issuing authority; False = not yet primary source verified. Required for NCQA and Joint Commission credentialing accreditation.']
  privileging_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential is directly relevant to the provider\'s hospital privileging determination. True = this credential is required or considered in the privileging decision; False = informational only (e.g., a CME activity not tied to a specific privilege).']
  psv_date date [note: 'The date on which primary source verification was completed for this credential. NCQA and The Joint Commission require re-verification at defined intervals (typically every 3 years). Drives re-verification scheduling in Symplr.']
  psv_method varchar [note: 'The method used to conduct primary source verification of this credential (e.g., online query to state medical board database, written confirmation from issuing authority, electronic query via NPDB or OIG). Documents the verification audit trail for accreditation surveys.']
  recredentialing_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the provider must complete recredentialing for this credential to maintain active status. NCQA and The Joint Commission require recredentialing at least every 3 years. Drives workflow queues in Symplr and credentialing management systems.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which the credential was most recently renewed or recertified by the issuing authority. Tracks the renewal history cycle for compliance reporting and credentialing re-verification workflows.']
  restriction_description varchar [note: 'Description of any restrictions, conditions, or limitations placed on this credential by the issuing authority (e.g., \'Supervised practice only\', \'No DEA Schedule II prescribing\', \'Probationary license\'). Null if no restrictions apply.']
  sam_exclusion_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAM.gov federal exclusions database was checked for this provider. SAM exclusion screening is required for providers participating in federal healthcare programs (Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this credential record was sourced or last updated (e.g., Symplr Credentialing, Epic, Cerner Millennium, MEDITECH Expanse, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and ETL audit trails in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this credential record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Symplr credential record ID, Epic provider record ID). Enables traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the source system for reconciliation and audit.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Granular classification within the credential type. For state licenses: MD, DO, RN, NP, PA, CRNA, PharmD, etc. For DEA registrations: Schedule II, III, IV, V authorization. For board certifications: ABMS, AOA, ABNS, or other recognized board specialty. For CME: Category 1 or Category 2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|RN|NP|PA|CRNA|PharmD|Schedule_II|Schedule_III|Schedule_IV|Schedule_V|ABMS|AOA|ABNS|CME_Cat1|CME_Cat2 — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this credential record was most recently modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer ETL pipeline and audit trail requirements under HIPAA and HITRUST.']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'Indicates the source used to verify this credential. Primary source verification (PSV) is required by NCQA, The Joint Commission, and CMS for credentialing. Secondary source verification uses intermediary databases. Self-reported credentials have not been independently verified.']

  Note: 'Master record of all professional credentials, licenses, certifications, registrations, and continuing education held by individual clinicians. Encompasses: (1) State medical licenses (MD, DO, RN, NP, PA) with license number, issuing state board, issue/expiration dates, status, and renewal history; (2) DEA controlled substance registrations with DEA number, schedule authorizations (II-V), state of registration, registration status, business activity type, and expiration date; (3) Specialty board certifications (ABMS, AOA, ABNS, and other recognized boards) with certifying board, specialty certified, certification number, initial certification date, expiration date, MOC (Maintenance of Certification) status, and recertification history; (4) CME (Continuing Medical Education) activity tracking with activity title, accrediting organization (AMA PRA, ACCME), activity type (live, enduring, performance improvement), credit hours, completion date, specialty relevance, and CME category (Category 1, Category 2). Tracks credential type, issuing authority, verification source, and primary source verification status for all credential types. Serves as the consolidated SSOT for all provider credentialing artifacts — no other product in this domain stores license, certification, DEA registration, board certification, or CME data. Supports HIPAA-compliant credentialing workflows, state licensure verification, board certification verification, DEA compliance, CME compliance for licensure renewal, and pharmacy order validation for controlled substances.'
}

Table provider.privileging {
  privileging_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each clinical privileging record granted to a clinician at a specific facility. Primary key for this data product.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Clinical privileges are audited for compliance with credentialing standards (TJC MS.06.01.05, CMS 482.12). Compliance teams audit privilege files for documentation of training, case volume, FPPE/OPPE,']
  care_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific hospital, clinic, or healthcare facility at which the clinical privileges are granted and exercised. Privileges are facility-specific per Joint Commission standards.']
  cpt_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: Joint Commission and CMS require hospitals to delineate specific CPT-coded procedures each clinician is privileged to perform. Privileging committees use CPT codes to define procedure-level authorizat']
  icd_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: Surgical and procedural privileges reference ICD-10-PCS procedure codes for delineation of clinical privileges. Medical staff offices use these codes to define procedure-specific authorization scope a']
  modality_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to radiology.modality. Business justification: Clinical privileges in radiology are modality-specific — a clinician may be privileged for MRI-guided procedures but not fluoroscopy. Linking privileging to modality supports credentialing committees,']
  privileging_clinician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed clinician or healthcare professional to whom clinical privileges are granted. Links to the provider master record.']
  privileging_proctor_provider_clinician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervising or proctoring physician assigned to observe and evaluate the clinician during the provisional privilege period. Required when is_provisional is True.']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.service. Business justification: Joint Commission Delineation of Clinical Privileges requires privileges to be tied to specific clinical service lines (e.g., cardiac surgery service). Medical Staff Offices track privilege grants by s']
  specialty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Clinical privileges are granted for specific specialties. privileging.specialty_code and specialty_name should reference specialty table as authoritative source.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the approving medical staff committee formally voted to grant or renew the clinical privileges. Distinct from the effective date, which may be retroactive or future-dated.']
  board_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether board certification in the relevant specialty is a prerequisite for granting this privilege per the facility\'s medical staff bylaws. True when board certification is required.']
  completed_case_volume varchar [note: 'Actual number of cases or procedures performed by the clinician under this privilege during the current privilege period. Used for ongoing competency assessment and reappointment decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this privileging record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA Security Rule audit control requirements.']
  delineation_form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privilege delineation form used to define the scope of this privilege grant. Tracks which version of the approved privilege form was in effect at the time of granting, supporting audit and accreditation review.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the clinical privilege grant becomes active and the clinician is authorized to perform the privileged procedures at the facility. Binding start date per medical staff bylaws.']
  emtala_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this privilege covers emergency on-call obligations under EMTALA (Emergency Medical Treatment and Labor Act). True when the clinician is authorized and obligated to respond to emergency department on-call requests under this privilege.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the clinical privilege grant expires and must be renewed through the reappointment process. Typically aligned with the medical staff reappointment cycle (commonly every 2 years per TJC standards).']
  fppe_completion_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE) was completed and findings were submitted to the medical staff committee. Null when FPPE is not required or not yet completed.']
  fppe_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Focused Professional Practice Evaluation (FPPE) is required for this privilege grant. FPPE is mandatory for all new privileges and triggered by performance concerns per TJC standards. True when FPPE is required.']
  is_provisional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the privilege is granted on a provisional basis, requiring supervision or proctoring during an initial evaluation period before full privileges are confirmed. True when provisional; False when full privileges are granted.']
  malpractice_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician\'s malpractice/professional liability insurance coverage has been verified as current and meeting the facility\'s minimum coverage requirements at the time of privilege grant. True when verified.']
  npdb_report_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the adverse action was reported to the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB). Must occur within 15 days of the adverse action per HCQIA requirements. Null when no NPDB report is required.']
  npdb_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an adverse action report to the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB) is required for this privilege action (e.g., suspension >30 days, revocation). True triggers mandatory NPDB reporting workflow.']
  oppe_last_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the most recent Ongoing Professional Practice Evaluation (OPPE) review for this privilege. OPPE must occur at regular intervals (at least every 12 months) per TJC standards to support reappointment.']
  peer_review_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or rating assigned during the most recent peer review or focused professional practice evaluation (FPPE) or ongoing professional practice evaluation (OPPE) for this privilege. Used to support reappointment decisions. Sensitive business-confidential data.']
  privilege_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the privilege type granted. Clinical covers general patient care; surgical covers operative procedures; procedural covers invasive non-surgical acts; diagnostic covers imaging/interpretation; prescriptive covers medication authority; administrative covers leadership roles.']
  privilege_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the scope and limitations of the clinical privilege, including any conditions, supervision requirements, or volume thresholds specified by the medical staff committee.']
  privilege_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the clinical privilege granted (e.g., \'Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy\', \'Cardiac Catheterization\', \'Endotracheal Intubation\'). Sourced from the facility\'s approved privilege delineation form.']
  privilege_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the medical staff office to uniquely reference this privileging grant. Used in correspondence, audit trails, and credentialing system lookups (e.g., Symplr privilege record number).']
  privilege_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the clinical privilege grant. Active indicates full authorization; provisional indicates supervised practice during initial period; suspended indicates temporary removal pending investigation; revoked indicates permanent removal; expired indicates lapsed without renewal; pending indicates awaiting committee approval.']
  privilege_type varchar [note: 'Specific classification of the privilege grant. Core privileges are standard for the specialty; special privileges require additional training; temporary privileges are time-limited; telemedicine covers remote care delivery; locum tenens covers temporary coverage providers; disaster privileges are emergency-activated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: core|special|temporary|telemedicine|locum_tenens|disaster|provisional — promote to reference product]']
  provisional_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the provisional privilege period ends and the clinician transitions to full privileges, contingent on satisfactory proctoring evaluation. Null when privilege is not provisional.']
  reappointment_cycle_years varchar [note: 'Number of years in the medical staff reappointment cycle for this privilege (typically 2 years per TJC standards). Determines the frequency of privilege renewal and competency review.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the clinician submitted the privilege request or reappointment application to the medical staff office. Used to track processing turnaround time and compliance with credentialing timelines.']
  required_case_volume varchar [note: 'Minimum number of cases or procedures the clinician must perform within the privilege period to demonstrate competency and maintain the privilege. Set by the medical staff committee based on specialty standards.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the clinical privilege was permanently revoked, if applicable. Null when privilege has not been revoked. Triggers mandatory NPDB adverse action report within 15 days.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for permanent privilege revocation, such as gross negligence, criminal conviction, license revocation, or sustained peer review findings. Sensitive business-confidential information required for NPDB adverse action reporting.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this privileging record in the originating operational system (e.g., Symplr privilege record ID, Epic credentialing record ID). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL traceability.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this privileging record originated (e.g., SYMPLR for Symplr Credentialing, EPIC for Epic OpTime/Cadence, CERNER for Cerner Millennium, MEDITECH for MEDITECH Expanse, MANUAL for paper-based entry).']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the clinical privilege was suspended, if applicable. Null when privilege has never been suspended. Required for adverse action reporting to the National Practitioner Data Bank (NPDB).']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for privilege suspension, such as patient safety concern, peer review finding, license lapse, or investigation pending. Sensitive business-confidential information used for adverse action documentation and NPDB reporting.']
  telemedicine_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this privilege extends to telemedicine or telehealth delivery of the authorized services. True when the clinician is authorized to perform the privileged activity via remote/virtual care modalities.']
  training_requirement_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinician has satisfied all required training, education, or competency documentation prerequisites for this privilege as defined on the privilege delineation form. True when all training requirements are verified.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this privileging record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental ETL processing for the Silver Layer lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Tracks clinical privileges granted to individual clinicians at specific facilities, authorizing them to perform defined procedures and services. Captures privilege category, procedure list, granting facility, privilege status (active, provisional, suspended, revoked), effective and expiration dates, and approving medical staff committee. Core to EMTALA compliance and Joint Commission accreditation.'
}

Table provider.network_affiliation {
  network_affiliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each provider network affiliation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  care_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific facility or practice location at which the provider participates in this network. A provider may have multiple network affiliations at different facility locations.']
  clinician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual clinician or organizational provider associated with this network affiliation record. Links to the provider master entity.']
  fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.fee_schedule. Business justification: Each network affiliation has an associated fee schedule governing reimbursement rates for that provider-payer-network combination. Replacing the denormalized reimbursement_model text with a proper FK ']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the medical group or group practice through which the individual provider participates in this network. Many payer contracts are held at the group level, with individual providers enrolled under the group.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.health_plan. Business justification: Network affiliations are health-plan-specific: a provider may be in-network for a payer\'s HMO but not their PPO. Member-facing directory accuracy, claims adjudication, and CMS network adequacy filings']
  org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Organizational providers have network affiliations in addition to individual clinicians. FK will be NULL for clinician-only affiliations. No redundant columns to remove.']
  payer_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the provider contract or participation agreement governing the terms of this network affiliation, including reimbursement rates and obligations. Links to the contract management system.']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health insurance payer or managed care organization that owns or administers the network to which the provider is affiliated.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific payer network or care network (ACO, HMO, PPO, POS) to which the provider is affiliated.']
  specialty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Network participation is specialty-specific. network_affiliation.specialty_code and specialty_description should reference specialty table.']
  accepts_new_patients boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is currently accepting new patients within this network. Distinct from panel_status in that it reflects the provider\'s current operational availability rather than the formal panel designation.']
  aco_participant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is a participating member of an Accountable Care Organization (ACO) under this network affiliation. ACO participation drives shared savings calculations and quality reporting under CMS MSSP and Next Gen ACO programs.']
  affiliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the provider\'s network affiliation. \'active\' indicates the provider is currently participating and eligible to see network patients. \'pending\' indicates enrollment is in process. \'suspended\' indicates temporary hold pending investigation or credentialing review.']
  age_range_max varchar [note: 'Maximum patient age (in years) that the provider accepts within this network affiliation. Null indicates no upper age limit. Used for provider directory filtering and patient-to-provider matching.']
  age_range_min varchar [note: 'Minimum patient age (in years) that the provider accepts within this network affiliation. Used for provider directory filtering and patient-to-provider matching in managed care systems.']
  credentialing_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the provider\'s credentialing with this network expires and re-credentialing must be completed. Typically occurs every two years per NCQA standards. Used to trigger re-credentialing workflows.']
  credentialing_status varchar [note: 'Current credentialing status of the provider with the payer or network organization. Credentialing must be completed before a provider can be listed as in-network. Tracked via Symplr or CAQH ProView.']
  directory_published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this provider network affiliation record has been published in the payer\'s public-facing provider directory. CMS requires accurate and up-to-date provider directories for all Medicare Advantage and Marketplace plans.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the provider\'s participation in the network becomes effective and the provider is eligible to see in-network patients. Used for claims adjudication and provider directory accuracy.']
  gender_served varchar [note: 'Indicates the patient gender population served by the provider within this network (e.g., all genders, male only, female only, pediatric). Used for provider directory accuracy and patient matching.']
  geographic_service_area varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic region or service area covered by this network affiliation (e.g., state name, county, metropolitan statistical area, or CMS-defined service area). Used for network adequacy geographic analysis and CMS service area filings.']
  handicap_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider\'s practice location associated with this network affiliation is handicap accessible per ADA requirements. Required for CMS provider directory accuracy and ADA compliance reporting.']
  hospital_affiliation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this network affiliation record pertains to a hospital or facility provider (as opposed to an individual clinician). Used to distinguish facility-level from practitioner-level network participation in directory and adequacy reporting.']
  languages_spoken varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages spoken by the provider at this network location, using ISO 639-1 language codes. Required for provider directory accuracy and CMS language access compliance under Section 1557 of the ACA.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the network affiliation information was last verified against the payer\'s provider directory or credentialing source. CMS requires provider directories to be updated at least every 90 days.']
  mips_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is eligible for Merit-based Incentive Payment System (MIPS) reporting under this network affiliation. MIPS eligibility affects payment adjustments under MACRA and is tracked at the provider-payer level.']
  network_adequacy_category varchar [note: 'Provider category used for CMS and state network adequacy analysis. Classifies the provider\'s role in meeting time-and-distance and appointment availability standards required for network certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary_care|specialist|behavioral_health|hospital|ancillary|pharmacy — promote to reference product if additional categories are needed]']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Tiered network designation indicating the provider\'s cost and quality tier within the payer\'s network structure. Tier 1 typically represents highest-value providers with lowest patient cost-sharing; higher tiers carry greater patient out-of-pocket costs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes or comments related to this network affiliation record, such as special contract terms, exceptions, or credentialing remarks entered by network management staff.']
  npi varchar [note: 'The 10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned by CMS to uniquely identify the provider participating in this network affiliation. Used for claims routing and provider directory publication.']
  panel_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of patients the provider is willing to accept within this network. Used for network adequacy analysis and patient assignment in managed care models such as HMO and ACO.']
  panel_current_count varchar [note: 'Current number of attributed or assigned patients on the provider\'s panel within this network. Used alongside panel_capacity to determine panel availability and support network adequacy reporting.']
  panel_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the provider\'s patient panel is open to new patients, closed, or accepting only limited new patients within this network. Critical for network adequacy reporting and patient access compliance required by CMS.']
  participation_type varchar [note: 'Defines the provider\'s participation classification within the network. Determines patient cost-sharing levels and reimbursement rates. \'preferred\' indicates a higher-tier in-network designation with lower patient cost-sharing.']
  primary_care_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is designated as a Primary Care Physician (PCP) within this network. PCPs serve as care coordinators in HMO and ACO models and are required for patient attribution and referral management.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network affiliation record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with HIPAA record retention requirements.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network affiliation record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  service_area_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the primary state in which the provider participates in this network. Used for state-level network adequacy reporting and licensure validation.']
  service_area_zip_code varchar [note: 'Primary ZIP code of the provider\'s practice location within this network affiliation. Used for geographic network adequacy analysis, time-and-distance standards compliance, and provider directory accuracy.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this affiliation record in the originating source system (e.g., Symplr affiliation ID, CAQH provider ID). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL traceability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this network affiliation record was sourced (e.g., Symplr credentialing platform, CAQH ProView, Epic, Cerner). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  telehealth_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the provider is authorized to deliver telehealth services to patients within this network affiliation. Relevant for network adequacy reporting and patient access under CMS telehealth expansion policies.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the provider\'s participation in the network ends. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active affiliation. Used for claims adjudication to validate in-network eligibility at date of service.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the termination of the provider\'s network affiliation. Used for network management analytics, OIG compliance monitoring, and understanding provider attrition patterns.']

  Note: 'Association record linking clinicians and organizational providers to payer networks, ACOs, HMOs, PPOs, and integrated care networks. Captures network name, network tier, participation type, effective and termination dates, panel status (open/closed), and geographic service area. Supports provider directory accuracy and network adequacy reporting required by CMS.'
}

Table provider.group {
  group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the provider group record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  npi_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.npi_registry. Business justification: Provider groups have Type 2 NPIs registered in the NPI registry.
