  hcpcs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.hcpcs_code. Business justification: PA rules govern HCPCS codes for DME, supplies, and specialty drugs. Critical for authorization workflow automation, step therapy enforcement, and utilization management in payer operations.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific health plan product under which this prior authorization rule applies.']
  icd_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: PA rules are frequently diagnosis-specific (e.g., PA for MRI only with ICD-10 M54.5 low back pain). Payer PA systems require ICD FK for clinical criteria matching, medical necessity determination, and']
  org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Prior auth rules are often facility-specific (e.g., a payer requires PA for inpatient admissions at a specific hospital system). Linking prior_auth_rule to org_provider enables facility-level PA requi']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance payer organization that enforces this prior authorization requirement.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Prior authorization rules must reference organizational clinical policies that define medical necessity criteria, coverage determination frameworks, and appeals processes. This linkage ensures PA rule']
  specialty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Prior authorization rules are specialty-specific (e.g., cardiology procedures, oncology treatments, orthopedic surgery). Critical for PA determination engines and clinical criteria application. Creati']
  superseded_prior_auth_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on prior_auth_rule (superseded_prior_auth_rule_id)']
  age_maximum varchar [note: 'Maximum patient age in years for which this prior authorization rule applies. Null if no age restriction.']
  age_minimum varchar [note: 'Minimum patient age in years for which this prior authorization rule applies. Null if no age restriction.']
  appeal_process_description varchar [note: 'Description of the process for appealing a prior authorization denial under this rule, including timelines and contact information.']
  auto_approval_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the criteria that must be met for automated approval of the prior authorization request.']
  auto_approval_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prior authorization rule supports automated approval based on predefined criteria without manual review.']
  clinical_criteria_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical guideline, medical policy, or evidence-based criteria document that defines the medical necessity standards for this prior authorization rule.']
  contact_fax varchar [note: 'Payer fax number for prior authorization document submission related to this rule.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Payer contact phone number for prior authorization inquiries and submissions related to this rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this prior authorization rule record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'List or description of clinical documentation that must be submitted with the prior authorization request (e.g., chart notes, lab results, imaging reports).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this prior authorization rule becomes active and enforceable by the payer.']
  exception_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of circumstances under which exceptions to the prior authorization requirement may be granted (e.g., emergency situations, continuity of care).']
  frequency_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the service can be performed within the frequency period without prior authorization. Null if no frequency limit applies.']
  frequency_limit_period_days varchar [note: 'Time period in days over which the frequency limit is measured. Null if no period applies.']
  gender_restriction varchar [note: 'Gender-specific applicability of the prior authorization rule. Values: male, female, all (no restriction), or other.']
  medical_policy_number varchar [note: 'Payer-specific medical policy document number or identifier that governs this prior authorization rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or special instructions related to this prior authorization rule.']
  pa_requirement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prior authorization requirement level: required (mandatory PA), not required, clinical review (medical necessity review), peer-to-peer (physician discussion), notification only, or retrospective review.']
  place_of_service_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit CMS Place of Service code identifying the location where the service is rendered (e.g., 11=office, 21=inpatient hospital, 22=outpatient hospital).']
  portal_url varchar [note: 'Web address of the payer portal for electronic prior authorization submission, if applicable.']
  prior_auth_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the prior authorization rule: active (in effect), inactive (not enforced), pending (scheduled for activation), suspended (temporarily paused), or retired (permanently discontinued).']
  quantity_limit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum quantity or units of service allowed without prior authorization under this rule. Null if no quantity limit applies.']
  quantity_limit_period_days varchar [note: 'Time period in days over which the quantity limit is measured (e.g., 30 days, 90 days). Null if no period applies.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Citation of the regulatory or statutory authority that permits or requires this prior authorization rule (e.g., CMS National Coverage Determination, state insurance law).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for this prior authorization rule, used for external reference and reporting.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the prior authorization rule for display and reporting purposes.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the healthcare service type subject to prior authorization (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, surgical, pharmacy, durable medical equipment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inpatient|outpatient|emergency|surgical|diagnostic|therapeutic|pharmacy|dme|home_health|behavioral_health — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system from which this prior authorization rule was ingested (e.g., Epic, Cerner, payer portal).']
  step_therapy_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the step therapy requirements, including which alternative treatments must be tried first and failure criteria.']
  step_therapy_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether step therapy (trial of alternative treatments before approval) is required as part of the prior authorization criteria.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Allowed method(s) for submitting the prior authorization request: payer portal, fax, phone, EDI 278 transaction, email, or postal mail.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this prior authorization rule is no longer active. Null indicates the rule is currently in effect with no planned end date.']
  turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Standard turnaround time in hours for the payer to respond to a prior authorization request under this rule.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last updated this prior authorization rule record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this prior authorization rule record was last modified.']
  urgent_turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Expedited turnaround time in hours for urgent or emergent prior authorization requests under this rule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining payer-specific prior authorization (PA) requirements for procedures, services, medications, and care settings. Captures payer ID, health plan ID, procedure code (CPT/HCPCS), diagnosis code applicability, service category, place of service, PA requirement type (required, not required, clinical review, peer-to-peer), clinical criteria reference, turnaround time standard (urgent vs standard), submission method (portal, fax, phone, EDI 278), effective date, and termination date. SSOT for PA requirement rules used by utilization management and order authorization workflows.'
}

Table insurance.utilization_review {
  utilization_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the utilization review record. Primary key.']
  appealed_utilization_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on utilization_review (appealed_utilization_review_id)']
  care_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the healthcare facility where the service or admission occurred.']
  clinical_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.clinical_order. Business justification: Concurrent and retrospective UR: utilization reviewers evaluate specific clinical orders (high-cost procedures, inpatient admissions) for medical necessity. UR decisions (approve/deny/modify) are made']
  clinician_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physician or provider who rendered the service being reviewed.']
  cpt_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.cpt_code. Business justification: UR reviews are procedure-specific (e.g., reviewing CPT 43239 upper GI endoscopy). Linking to cpt_code enables clinical criteria lookup by procedure, UR decision reporting by CPT category, and medical ']
  drg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.drg. Business justification: Inpatient concurrent UR is fundamentally DRG-based — approved length of stay and payment are tied to the DRG grouping. UR directors require this FK for inpatient review workflows, LOS benchmarking aga']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.health_plan. Business justification: Utilization review is conducted under the rules of a specific health plan — benefit limits, coverage tiers, and authorization requirements all vary by plan. The existing payer_id FK identifies the pay']
  icd_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: UR decisions (admit/deny/modify) are driven by ICD-10 diagnosis codes — clinical criteria (InterQual/MCG) are applied against the diagnosis. UR nurses and medical directors require this FK for clinica']
  insurance_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.insurance_coverage. Business justification: Utilization review is conducted against a patient\'s specific insurance coverage to apply applicable benefits, prior auth criteria, and medical necessity standards. Linking utilization_review to insura']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health plan member (patient) whose care is being reviewed.']
  org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Utilization review is conducted against the org_provider (hospital/health system) whose admission or services are under review. UR reporting, appeals tracking, and CMS compliance require identifying t']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insurance payer or health plan conducting or requesting the utilization review.']
  prior_auth_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.prior_auth_rule. Business justification: Utilization review activities are conducted against specific prior authorization rules — the UR reviewer applies the PA rule criteria to determine medical necessity. Linking utilization_review to prio']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Utilization review activities must comply with specific compliance programs: UM compliance program, clinical criteria adherence program, regulatory timeframe compliance. Linking UR to governing compli']
  referral_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.referral_order. Business justification: Referral utilization management: UR reviews are frequently triggered by referral orders requiring specialist or facility authorization. Managed care organizations require this link to track UR decisio']
  specialty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Utilization review applies specialty-specific clinical criteria (e.g., cardiology UR criteria, oncology treatment protocols). Essential for peer-to-peer matching, clinical appropriateness review, and ']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.unit. Business justification: Inpatient utilization review by clinical unit: UR continued-stay reviews apply unit-specific clinical criteria — ICU vs. step-down vs. med-surg units have different approved LOS benchmarks and acuity ']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical encounter (admission, visit, or service episode) subject to utilization review.']
  admission_date date [note: 'Date the patient was admitted to the facility, applicable for inpatient utilization reviews.']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal was filed by the member or provider in response to the utilization review decision.']
  appeal_rights_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member and provider were notified of their rights to appeal the utilization review decision, as required by ERISA and state regulations.']
  approved_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of inpatient days approved by the utilization review, which may differ from the requested length of stay.']
  clinical_criteria_applied varchar [note: 'Name of the clinical guideline or criteria set used to evaluate medical necessity (e.g., InterQual, MCG, Milliman Care Guidelines).']
  clinical_documentation_reviewed varchar [note: 'Summary or list of clinical documentation reviewed during the UM process (e.g., H&P, progress notes, lab results, imaging reports).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the utilization review record was first created in the system.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version number or edition of the clinical criteria applied during the review.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for denial or modification of the service request (e.g., not medically necessary, experimental treatment, lack of documentation).']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the reason for denial or modification, provided to the member and provider.']
  discharge_date date [note: 'Date the patient was discharged from the facility, applicable for inpatient utilization reviews.']
  medical_record_number varchar [note: 'Patient\'s medical record number at the facility where the service was provided.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify the member and provider of the review decision (mail, fax, email, patient portal, phone).']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date the review decision notification was sent to the member and provider.']
  peer_to_peer_date date [note: 'Date the peer-to-peer review discussion occurred between the treating provider and the payer\'s medical reviewer.']
  peer_to_peer_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treating provider requested a peer-to-peer discussion with the medical director or physician reviewer to appeal or discuss the initial review decision.']
  place_of_service_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit code indicating the setting where the service was provided (e.g., 21 for inpatient hospital, 11 for office).']
  regulatory_timeframe_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the utilization review was completed within the regulatory timeframe mandated by state or federal law (e.g., 72 hours for urgent pre-service, 30 days for retrospective).']
  rendering_provider_npi varchar [note: 'Ten-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the rendering provider.']
  requested_length_of_stay varchar [note: 'Number of inpatient days requested or initially authorized for the admission.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date the utilization review was completed and a final decision was rendered.']
  review_decision varchar [note: 'Final determination of the utilization review: approved (medical necessity met), denied (not medically necessary), modified (approved with changes), pended (additional information required), or partially approved.']
  review_initiation_date date [note: 'Date the utilization review process was initiated or the review request was received.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or case number assigned to this utilization review for tracking and reference by payers, providers, and UM teams.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the utilization review process.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Type of utilization management review conducted: pre-certification (prospective), concurrent review (during stay), retrospective review (post-service), discharge planning, or continued stay review.']
  reviewer_credentials varchar [note: 'Professional credentials of the reviewer (e.g., RN, MD, DO, NP) to demonstrate clinical qualifications for conducting the review.']
  reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the clinical reviewer documenting the rationale for the review decision.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date of the service, procedure, or admission being reviewed for medical necessity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application from which the utilization review record originated (e.g., Epic Healthy Planet, Cerner UM module, third-party UM vendor system).']
  turnaround_time_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours elapsed between review initiation and completion, used to measure UM process efficiency and compliance with regulatory timeframes.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last updated the utilization review record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the utilization review record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of utilization management (UM) review activities conducted by payers or internal UM teams to evaluate medical necessity for inpatient admissions, continued stays, procedures, and high-cost services. Captures review type (pre-certification, concurrent review, retrospective review, discharge planning), payer ID, member ID, admission/service date, clinical criteria applied (InterQual, MCG), review decision (approved, denied, modified, pended), denial reason code, peer-to-peer review request flag, appeal rights notification, and reviewer credentials. Supports UM compliance, denial management, and length-of-stay optimization.'
}

Table insurance.eligibility_span {
  eligibility_span_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the eligibility span record. Primary key for the eligibility span entity.']
  clinician_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the primary care physician assigned to or selected by the member. Populated when pcp_required_indicator is true.']
  eligibility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the X12 270/271 eligibility verification transaction that confirmed or established this eligibility span. Links to real-time eligibility verification events.']
  employer_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.employer_group. Business justification: Eligibility spans for group-sponsored members are tied to a specific employer group — the group determines benefit period dates, contribution amounts, and COBRA eligibility. The existing group_number ']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific health plan product under which this eligibility span is established. Defines the benefit structure and coverage rules.']
  insurance_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.insurance_coverage. Business justification: Eligibility spans (insurance-domain) and insurance_coverage (patient-domain) represent the same coverage from two system perspectives. Linking eligibility_span to insurance_coverage enables bidirectio']
  member_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.member_enrollment. Business justification: An eligibility span represents a continuous period of coverage that is directly derived from a member enrollment record. Linking eligibility_span to member_enrollment creates the authoritative chain f']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the member (patient) who holds this insurance coverage. Links to the patient domain master record.']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance payer organization responsible for adjudicating and paying claims under this coverage.']
  prior_eligibility_span_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on eligibility_span (prior_eligibility_span_id)']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the provider network associated with this eligibility span. Defines which providers are in-network for this coverage.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the primary subscriber (policyholder) of the insurance plan. May be the same as member_id if the member is the subscriber, or different if the member is a dependent.']
  benefit_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the benefit period for deductible and out-of-pocket maximum accumulation. Deductibles and accumulators reset after this date.']
  benefit_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the benefit period for deductible and out-of-pocket maximum accumulation. Typically aligns with plan year or calendar year.']
  cobra_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eligibility span represents COBRA continuation coverage. True if the member is continuing coverage under COBRA provisions after a qualifying event.']
  cobra_qualifying_event varchar [note: 'The qualifying event that triggered COBRA continuation coverage eligibility. Populated only when cobra_indicator is true.']
  cobra_qualifying_event_date date [note: 'The date on which the COBRA qualifying event occurred. Used to determine COBRA continuation period limits.']
  coordination_of_benefits_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether coordination of benefits rules apply because the member has multiple insurance coverages. True if this is a secondary or tertiary payer.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'The tier of coverage indicating whether the plan covers only the subscriber or includes dependents.']
  coverage_order varchar [note: 'Indicates the order of liability when multiple coverages exist. Primary coverage pays first; secondary and tertiary coverages pay after primary according to COB rules.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'The type of healthcare services covered under this eligibility span. Defines the scope of benefits available to the member.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this eligibility span record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail for record creation.']
  dual_eligibility_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is dually eligible for both Medicare and Medicaid. True if the member qualifies for both programs simultaneously.']
  dual_eligibility_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of dual eligibility status. QMB (Qualified Medicare Beneficiary), SLMB (Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary), QI (Qualifying Individual), QDWI (Qualified Disabled and Working Individual). Populated only when dual_eligibility_indicator is true.']
  eligibility_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the member\'s insurance coverage terminates under this eligibility span. Null indicates open-ended active coverage.']
  eligibility_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the member\'s insurance coverage becomes effective under this eligibility span. Represents the beginning of the continuous eligibility period.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility span. Active indicates coverage is in force; terminated indicates coverage has ended; suspended indicates temporary hold; COBRA indicates continuation coverage under COBRA provisions; pending indicates coverage not yet effective.']
  eligibility_verification_date date [note: 'The date on which eligibility was last verified with the payer through a 270/271 transaction or other verification method.']
  eligibility_verification_status varchar [note: 'The status of the most recent eligibility verification attempt. Verified indicates successful confirmation; unverified indicates no recent verification; failed indicates verification was attempted but unsuccessful.']
  enrollment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the enrollment was submitted. Distinguishes between online enrollment, paper application, phone enrollment, in-person enrollment, or automatic enrollment.']
  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'The source or channel through which the member enrolled in this coverage. Indicates whether enrollment was through an employer, exchange, direct purchase, broker, or government program.']
  exchange_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this coverage was purchased through a health insurance exchange (marketplace) established under the Affordable Care Act.']
  medicaid_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has Medicaid coverage. True if the member is enrolled in a state Medicaid program.']
  medicare_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has Medicare coverage. True if the member is enrolled in Medicare Part A, B, C, or D.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this eligibility span. Used to capture context not represented in structured fields.']
  pcp_assignment_date date [note: 'The date on which the current primary care physician was assigned to or selected by the member.']
  pcp_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is required to select and maintain a primary care physician under this plan. True for HMO and POS plans that require PCP designation.']
  premium_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The premium amount charged for this coverage, typically per billing period. Represents the member or employer contribution for maintaining coverage.']
  premium_payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which premium payments are due for this coverage.']
  prior_authorization_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certain services under this plan require prior authorization from the payer before services are rendered.']
  referral_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member must obtain a referral from their PCP before seeing a specialist. True for HMO plans with referral requirements.']
  relationship_to_subscriber varchar [note: 'Defines the relationship of the member to the primary subscriber. Determines dependent status and eligibility rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self|spouse|child|other_dependent|life_partner|domestic_partner|parent|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which this eligibility span record originated. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  subsidy_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of premium subsidy or tax credit applied to reduce the member\'s premium cost. Applicable for exchange plans with Advanced Premium Tax Credits (APTC).']
  subsidy_type varchar [note: 'The type of subsidy applied to this coverage. APTC (Advanced Premium Tax Credit), CSR (Cost-Sharing Reduction), employer subsidy, state subsidy, or none.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for termination of the eligibility span, if applicable. Populated only when eligibility_status is terminated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|involuntary|employment_ended|non_payment|death|divorce|age_limit|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user or system identifier that last updated this eligibility span record. Used for audit and accountability purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this eligibility span record was last modified. Represents the audit trail for record updates.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a continuous period of insurance eligibility for a member under a specific health plan, serving as the SSOT for eligibility determination within the insurance domain. Captures member ID, subscriber ID, payer ID, health plan ID, group number, eligibility start date, eligibility end date, eligibility status (active, terminated, suspended, COBRA), coverage type (medical, dental, vision, pharmacy, behavioral health), Medicare/Medicaid dual eligibility indicator, and the 270/271 eligibility transaction reference. Distinct from patient.patient_eligibility_verification which tracks real-time verification transactions.'
}

Table insurance.accumulator {
  accumulator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accumulator record. Primary key for tracking member benefit accumulation.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the health plan under which benefits are being accumulated. References the plan configuration and benefit structure.']
  insurance_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.insurance_coverage. Business justification: Accumulators (deductible, OOP max) are tracked at the patient coverage level. Linking accumulator to insurance_coverage enables real-time patient cost estimation, point-of-service cost-sharing calcula']
  member_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.member_enrollment. Business justification: Accumulators track year-to-date deductible and out-of-pocket accumulation for a member under a specific enrollment. The accumulator resets with each enrollment period and is scoped to the enrollment\'s']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the health plan member whose benefits are being accumulated. Links to the member master record.']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance payer organization responsible for the health plan and benefit adjudication.']
  prior_accumulator_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on accumulator (prior_accumulator_id)']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the primary subscriber of the health plan. May differ from member_id for dependents.']
  accumulated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The year-to-date dollar amount or count that has been accumulated toward the benefit threshold. Represents member cost-sharing contributions or service utilization.']
  accumulator_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the accumulator: active (accumulating), suspended (temporarily paused), closed (benefit period ended), or pending reset (awaiting new benefit year initialization).']
  accumulator_type varchar [note: 'The category of benefit accumulation being tracked: individual deductible, family deductible, individual out-of-pocket (OOP) maximum, family OOP maximum, visit limit, or service-specific dollar limit.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total dollar amount of manual adjustments applied to this accumulator due to claim reversals, appeals, or administrative corrections. May be positive or negative.']
  adjustment_count varchar [note: 'The number of adjustment transactions applied to this accumulator. Used for audit trail and data quality monitoring.']
  benefit_period_end_date date [note: 'The date when the benefit accumulation period ends. Typically December 31 or the plan termination date.']
  benefit_period_start_date date [note: 'The date when the benefit accumulation period begins. Typically January 1 or the plan effective date for mid-year enrollments.']
  benefit_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which the accumulator is tracking benefit utilization. Accumulators typically reset annually on the plan renewal date.']
  carryover_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount carried over from the previous benefit period, if the plan allows deductible or OOP carryover for services rendered near year-end.']
  carryover_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this accumulator includes carryover amounts from a prior benefit period. True if carryover_amount is greater than zero.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'The tier of coverage for which the accumulator applies: individual, family, employee plus spouse, employee plus children, or full family.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this accumulator record was first created in the system. Typically at the start of a new benefit period or upon member enrollment.']
  cross_accumulation_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether amounts in this accumulator also count toward other accumulator types. For example, deductible amounts may also count toward OOP max.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this accumulator. Typically USD for US-based healthcare plans.']
  embedded_deductible_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this family-level accumulator uses embedded individual deductibles. True means each family member has an individual deductible within the family deductible.']
  last_adjustment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent adjustment transaction applied to this accumulator. Null if no adjustments have been made.']
  last_claim_date date [note: 'Service date of the most recent claim that updated this accumulator. Used for recency tracking and accumulator aging analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this accumulator record was last modified. Critical for real-time benefit calculation and member portal display.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the accumulator tracks in-network services, out-of-network services, or a combined bucket for both network types.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special circumstances, or administrative notes related to this accumulator. May include explanations for manual adjustments or unusual accumulation patterns.']
  plan_year_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential counter indicating which plan year this accumulator represents for the member. Value of 1 for first year, 2 for second year, etc. Used for multi-year trend analysis.']
  remaining_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The remaining balance before the threshold is met. Calculated as threshold_amount minus accumulated_amount. Critical for member cost-sharing estimates.']
  reset_date date [note: 'The date when this accumulator was last reset to zero, typically at the start of a new benefit period or upon plan change.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'The category of healthcare services to which this accumulator applies: medical, pharmacy, dental, vision, behavioral health, or all services combined.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the operational system that originated this accumulator record. Examples: Epic Resolute, Cerner Revenue Cycle, or proprietary claims adjudication platform.']
  threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The plan-defined maximum amount or limit for this accumulator type. Once reached, benefit rules change (e.g., deductible met, OOP max reached).']
  threshold_met_date date [note: 'The date on which the member first met the benefit threshold. Null if threshold has not been met. Used for benefit transition tracking and member communication.']
  threshold_met_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the member has met or exceeded the benefit threshold for this accumulator type. True when accumulated_amount >= threshold_amount.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'The total number of claim transactions that have contributed to this accumulator balance. Used for utilization pattern analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking a member\'s year-to-date accumulation of deductible, out-of-pocket maximum, and benefit limit utilization against their health plan thresholds. Captures member ID, health plan ID, benefit year, accumulator type (individual deductible, family deductible, individual OOP max, family OOP max, visit limit, dollar limit), in-network vs out-of-network bucket, accumulated amount to date, plan threshold amount, remaining balance, last updated date, and the claim transactions contributing to the accumulation. Critical for member cost-sharing calculation and benefit exhaustion determination.'
}

Table insurance.capitation_contract {
  capitation_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capitation contract record. Primary key for the capitation contract entity.']
  care_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.care_site. Business justification: Capitation contract facility attribution: capitation arrangements assign attributed member populations to specific facilities for PMPM rate calculation and settlement reporting. Identifying the care s']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.provider_group. Business justification: Capitation contracts are executed with provider groups for PMPM payments and risk-sharing arrangements. Essential for VBC contract management, payment reconciliation, and performance tracking. No exis']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.health_plan. Business justification: Capitation contracts define per-member-per-month payment rates for a specific health plan\'s attributed population. The PMPM rate, quality withhold, and risk corridor thresholds are all plan-specific. ']
  org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Capitation contracts executed with organizational providers (hospitals, health systems) for bundled payments and episode-based reimbursement. Critical for hospital VBC arrangements and payment reconci']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance payer or health plan organization that is party to this capitation contract.']
  provider_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.provider_network. Business justification: Capitation contracts are scoped to a specific provider network — the attributed member population is defined by the network\'s geographic service area and provider roster. Linking capitation_contract t']
  renewed_capitation_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on capitation_contract (renewed_capitation_contract_id)']
  specialty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.specialty. Business justification: Specialty-specific capitation contracts (e.g., behavioral health carve-outs, cardiology bundles) are standard in value-based care. Linking capitation_contract to specialty enables network adequacy rep']
  attributed_member_count varchar [note: 'The current number of members attributed to this capitation contract for payment calculation purposes.']
  attributed_population_definition varchar [note: 'Textual description of the criteria and methodology used to define which members are attributed to this capitation contract for payment and performance measurement purposes.']
  auto_assignment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can be automatically assigned or attributed to this capitation contract based on utilization patterns and attribution logic.']
  benchmark_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology used to establish the cost and quality benchmarks against which performance is measured for shared savings and risk calculations.']
  carve_out_services varchar [note: 'List or description of specific high-cost or specialized services carved out from the capitation arrangement and reimbursed separately (e.g., transplants, oncology, behavioral health).']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the capitation contract for identification and reporting purposes.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the capitation contract, used for reference in billing, claims, and financial reporting.']
  contract_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or department within the healthcare organization responsible for managing this capitation contract.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'The date on which the capitation contract was executed and signed by authorized representatives of both parties.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capitation contract indicating its operational state and enforceability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|terminated|expired|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capitation arrangement defining the scope of services covered under the per-member-per-month payment structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global_capitation|professional_capitation|specialty_capitation|partial_capitation|primary_care_capitation|hospital_capitation — promote to reference product]']
  covered_services_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive textual description of the scope of clinical services and care management activities covered under the capitation payment arrangement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this capitation contract record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the capitation contract becomes binding and payment arrangements take effect.']
  excluded_services_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of services explicitly excluded from the capitation arrangement and subject to separate fee-for-service or other payment mechanisms.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number assigned by the regulatory authority for the contract filing submission.']
  geographic_service_area varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic region, counties, or zip codes covered by this capitation contract for member attribution and service delivery.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent amendment or modification to the capitation contract terms.']
  maximum_shared_loss_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum dollar amount of shared losses that the healthcare organization is responsible for in a performance period, expressed in USD.']
  maximum_shared_savings_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum dollar amount of shared savings that the healthcare organization can earn in a performance period, expressed in USD.']
  minimum_savings_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of cost savings below the benchmark that must be achieved before shared savings payments are triggered, expressed as a percentage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the capitation contract administration and management.']
  performance_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year during which performance is measured for quality metrics and financial reconciliation under this capitation contract.']
  pmpm_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the PMPM rate. Typically USD for US healthcare organizations.']
  pmpm_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'The fixed monthly payment amount per attributed member that the payer pays to the healthcare organization under the capitation arrangement, expressed in USD.']
  quality_measure_set varchar [note: 'The standardized set of quality performance measures used to evaluate care delivery and determine quality withhold return and incentive payments (e.g., HEDIS, MIPS, ACO quality measures).']
  quality_withhold_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total dollar amount withheld from capitation payments for quality performance measurement, expressed in USD.']
  quality_withhold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of capitation payments withheld by the payer pending achievement of quality performance targets, to be returned upon meeting quality benchmarks.']
  regulatory_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capitation contract requires filing with state insurance departments or CMS for regulatory approval and oversight.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract is eligible for or scheduled for renewal negotiation.']
  risk_adjustment_methodology varchar [note: 'The clinical risk stratification model used to adjust capitation payments based on member acuity and complexity. HCC (Hierarchical Condition Category) is the most common model in Medicare and commercial populations.']
  risk_corridor_lower_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The lower percentage threshold defining the risk corridor below which the healthcare organization is protected from losses. Expressed as a percentage of target costs.']
  risk_corridor_upper_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The upper percentage threshold defining the risk corridor above which the healthcare organization shares in savings. Expressed as a percentage of target costs.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which financial reconciliation and settlement of capitation payments, shared savings, and quality withholds occurs between the payer and healthcare organization.']
  shared_loss_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of cost overruns above the target benchmark that the healthcare organization is responsible for under the risk-sharing arrangement.']
  shared_savings_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of cost savings below the target benchmark that the healthcare organization retains as shared savings under the value-based care arrangement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the operational system from which this capitation contract record originated (e.g., Epic Resolute, Cerner Revenue Cycle, contract management system).']
  stop_loss_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'The per-member cost threshold above which stop-loss insurance or reinsurance protection applies to limit the healthcare organizations financial exposure for high-cost cases, expressed in USD.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which the capitation contract ends or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended or evergreen contracts.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the capitation contract.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last updated this capitation contract record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this capitation contract record was last modified in the system.']
  vbc_model varchar [note: 'The value-based care payment model framework governing financial risk and reward sharing between the healthcare organization and payer.']

  Note: 'Master record for capitation and value-based care (VBC) contracts between the healthcare organization and payers, defining per-member-per-month (PMPM) payment arrangements, risk pool structures, and shared savings/risk parameters. Captures contract ID, payer ID, capitation type (global capitation, professional capitation, specialty capitation), attributed population definition, PMPM rate, risk adjustment methodology (HCC — Hierarchical Condition Category), risk corridor parameters, quality withhold percentage, shared savings/loss split, contract effective dates, and settlement frequency. Supports VBC financial modeling and capitation payment reconciliation.'
}

Table insurance.capitation_payment {
  capitation_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capitation payment transaction record. Primary key for the capitation payment entity.']
  adjusted_capitation_payment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on capitation_payment (adjusted_capitation_payment_id)']
  capitation_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the capitation or value-based care contract governing this payment arrangement.']
  care_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary service location or facility receiving the capitation payment for the attributed member population.']
  group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provider group or practice organization receiving the capitation payment under the value-based care arrangement.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.health_plan. Business justification: Capitation payments are made for attributed members under a specific health plan. While capitation_contract_id provides indirect access to the plan, direct health_plan_id on capitation_payment enables']
  org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Capitation payments are disbursed directly to org_providers (hospital systems, IPAs). Financial reconciliation and revenue recognition reporting require tracking which org_provider received each capit']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the insurance payer or health plan making the capitation payment under the value-based care contract.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional positive or negative adjustments applied to the capitation payment for retroactive attribution changes, prior period corrections, or contract amendments.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for any adjustment amount applied to the capitation payment, including retroactive attribution changes or contract modifications.']
  attributed_member_count varchar [note: 'The number of health plan members attributed to the provider organization for the payment period under the capitation contract.']
  contract_year varchar [note: 'The contract year of the capitation agreement to which this payment applies, used for multi-year contract tracking and performance measurement.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'The organizational cost center or department to which the capitation payment revenue is allocated for internal financial management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this capitation payment record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the capitation payment. Typically USD for US healthcare organizations.']
  days_to_payment varchar [note: 'The number of days between the payment due date and the actual payment received date, used to track payer payment timeliness performance.']
  general_ledger_account varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which the capitation payment revenue is posted for financial reporting and accounting purposes.']
  gross_capitation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total gross capitation payment amount calculated as attributed member count multiplied by PMPM rate, before any adjustments or withholds.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The final net capitation payment amount received after all adjustments, risk factors, and quality withholds have been applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the capitation payment, including special circumstances, disputes, or follow-up actions required.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The contractually agreed date by which the payer is required to remit the capitation payment to the provider organization.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or mechanism used by the payer to remit the capitation payment.']
  payment_period_month varchar [note: 'The month (1-12) for which this capitation payment applies, representing the coverage period for attributed members.']
  payment_period_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which this capitation payment applies, representing the coverage period for attributed members.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the capitation payment was actually received by the provider organization, used for cash flow tracking and revenue recognition.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External payment reference number or remittance identifier provided by the payer for reconciliation and tracking purposes.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capitation payment transaction indicating processing state and reconciliation progress.']
  pmpm_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The contracted capitation rate paid per attributed member per month, expressed in dollars. This is the base rate before adjustments.']
  quality_withhold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The dollar amount withheld by the payer from the capitation payment pending achievement of quality performance metrics under the value-based care contract.']
  quality_withhold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the gross capitation amount withheld for quality performance, typically ranging from 0% to 20%.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the capitation payment was successfully reconciled against expected amounts and attributed member rosters.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation process comparing the received capitation payment against expected amounts based on attributed member counts and contract terms.']
  remittance_advice_date date [note: 'The date on which the remittance advice document was received from the payer, used for payment reconciliation and audit trail purposes.']
  remittance_advice_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal remittance advice document (835 ERA or paper EOB) was received from the payer with the capitation payment.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the capitation payment revenue is recognized in the general ledger according to GAAP revenue recognition principles.']
  revenue_recognition_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (month and year) in which the capitation payment revenue is recognized for financial reporting purposes.']
  risk_adjusted_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The capitation payment amount after applying the risk adjustment factor to account for population health complexity.']
  risk_adjustment_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'The risk adjustment multiplier applied to the capitation payment based on the attributed population\'s health acuity and complexity. Values typically range from 0.5 to 3.0.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which this capitation payment record originated, such as Epic Resolute or Cerner Revenue Cycle.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or system account that last modified this capitation payment record, used for audit trail and accountability purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this capitation payment record was last modified, used for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The dollar difference between the expected capitation payment and the actual payment received, used to identify discrepancies requiring investigation.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any variance between expected and actual capitation payment amounts, including attribution differences or rate discrepancies.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of monthly capitation payments received from payers under capitation and value-based care contracts. Captures payment period (month/year), payer ID, capitation contract ID, attributed member count, PMPM rate applied, gross capitation amount, risk adjustment factor, quality withhold amount, net payment amount, payment receipt date, payment method (EFT, check), payer remittance reference, and reconciliation status. Enables capitation revenue recognition, attributed population reconciliation, and VBC financial performance tracking.'
}

Table insurance.risk_adjustment {
  risk_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the risk adjustment submission record.']
  clinician_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the healthcare provider who documented the diagnosis codes submitted for risk adjustment.']
  corrected_risk_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on risk_adjustment (corrected_risk_adjustment_id)']
  diagnosis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_diagnosis. Business justification: Risk adjustment submissions must reference source clinical diagnoses for RADV audits and medical record validation. CMS requires linking HCC codes back to documented diagnoses. Essential for Medicare ']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific health plan under which the member is enrolled and for which risk adjustment applies.']
  icd_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.icd_code. Business justification: HCC risk adjustment maps ICD-10 diagnosis codes to HCC categories to compute risk scores for CMS payment. Risk adjustment analysts require this FK for RADV audit validation, diagnosis submission recon']
  mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the health plan member whose diagnoses are being submitted for risk adjustment.']
  org_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to provider.org_provider. Business justification: Risk adjustment submissions track which organizational provider documented the HCC diagnosis for RADV audit trails and medical record retrieval. Essential for Medicare Advantage and ACA risk adjustmen']
  payer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance payer organization responsible for the risk adjustment submission.']
  visit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the clinical encounter that generated the diagnosis codes submitted for risk adjustment.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'The date on which CMS or the payer accepted this risk adjustment submission as valid and included it in payment calculations.']
  chart_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the medical chart supporting this diagnosis was reviewed for risk adjustment coding accuracy and completeness.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'The CMS-assigned contract identifier for the Medicare Advantage or Part D plan under which this risk adjustment submission is made.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this risk adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  data_collection_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the period during which diagnosis data was collected for this risk adjustment submission, typically December 31 of the service year.']
  data_collection_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the period during which diagnosis data was collected for this risk adjustment submission, typically January 1 of the service year.']
  deleted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this risk adjustment submission has been deleted or voided, typically due to coding errors or medical record review findings.']
  deletion_date date [note: 'The date on which this risk adjustment submission was deleted or voided from the payment calculation.']
  deletion_reason varchar [note: 'The explanation for why this risk adjustment submission was deleted, such as coding error, insufficient documentation, or duplicate submission.']
  demographic_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The portion of the total risk score attributable to demographic factors such as age, gender, Medicaid eligibility, and disability status.']
  diagnosis_present_on_admission boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the diagnosis was present at the time of inpatient admission, required for certain risk adjustment and quality reporting programs.']
  diagnosis_type varchar [note: 'The classification of the diagnosis within the encounter documentation, indicating whether it was the principal reason for the encounter or a secondary condition.']
  disease_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The portion of the total risk score attributable to the submitted HCC categories representing chronic and acute conditions.']
  hcc_code varchar [note: 'The HCC category code to which the submitted diagnosis maps, representing a clinically and financially homogeneous group of conditions used in risk score calculation.']
  hcc_description varchar [note: 'The descriptive label for the HCC category, providing clinical context for the condition group (e.g., Diabetes with Chronic Complications, Congestive Heart Failure).']
  interaction_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The portion of the total risk score attributable to disease interaction factors, where the presence of multiple conditions results in higher expected costs than the sum of individual conditions.']
  medical_record_review_status varchar [note: 'The status of medical record documentation review supporting this diagnosis submission, critical for RADV audit compliance and internal quality assurance.']
  model varchar [note: 'The specific risk adjustment methodology applied to calculate the risk score. CMS-HCC (Hierarchical Condition Category) for Medicare Advantage, RxHCC for Part D, HHS-HCC for ACA marketplace plans, ESRD for end-stage renal disease, PACE for Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly, CDPS for Chronic Illness and Disability Payment System. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CMS-HCC|RxHCC|HHS-HCC|ESRD|PACE|CDPS|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  model_version varchar [note: 'The version year or release identifier of the risk adjustment model used for this submission (e.g., V24, V28 for CMS-HCC models).']
  payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of risk adjustment payment associated with this submission, calculated by multiplying the risk score by the base payment rate for the plan and geographic area.']
  payment_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The incremental payment impact of this specific HCC submission compared to baseline, representing the marginal revenue contribution of capturing this diagnosis.']
  payment_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which the risk adjustment payment applies, typically the year following the data collection year.']
  plan_benefit_package varchar [note: 'The specific benefit package identifier within the contract that applies to this member and risk adjustment submission.']
  radv_audit_date date [note: 'The date on which this submission was selected for or completed a RADV audit by CMS.']
  radv_audit_status varchar [note: 'The status of this submission in the CMS Risk Adjustment Data Validation audit process, which validates the accuracy of submitted diagnosis codes through medical record review.']
  recapture_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this diagnosis represents a recapture of a chronic condition that was documented in a prior year but not yet documented in the current payment year.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'The date on which CMS or the payer rejected this risk adjustment submission due to validation errors or policy violations.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'The detailed explanation provided by CMS or the payer for why this risk adjustment submission was rejected, including error codes and corrective action guidance.']
  risk_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'The calculated risk score (also known as RAF - Risk Adjustment Factor) for the member based on demographic factors and submitted HCC categories. A score of 1.0 represents average risk; higher scores indicate higher expected costs.']
  service_date date [note: 'The date of service on which the diagnosis was documented during the clinical encounter, used to determine eligibility for the risk adjustment payment year.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the operational system from which this risk adjustment submission originated, such as Epic EHR, Cerner Millennium, or a specialized risk adjustment platform.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this risk adjustment submission was transmitted to CMS EDGE server or payer risk adjustment system.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this risk adjustment submission, used for tracking and reconciliation with CMS or payer systems.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'The current processing status of the risk adjustment submission in the workflow from creation through final reconciliation with CMS or payer systems.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this risk adjustment submission was transmitted, used for audit trail and reconciliation purposes.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'The type of risk adjustment submission. Initial for first-time submission, Supplemental for additional diagnoses, Deletion for removing previously submitted diagnoses, Correction for fixing errors, RAPS for Risk Adjustment Processing System (legacy), EDPS for Encounter Data Processing System (current).']
  suspect_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this diagnosis has been identified as a suspect condition that should be documented and submitted for risk adjustment but has not yet been confirmed in the current year.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this risk adjustment record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of risk adjustment submissions and reconciliations under CMS risk adjustment programs (HCC — Hierarchical Condition Category for Medicare Advantage, EDGE server submissions for ACA plans) and payer-specific risk adjustment methodologies. Captures member ID, payer ID, health plan ID, risk adjustment model (CMS-HCC, RxHCC, HHS-HCC), diagnosis codes submitted (HCC-mapped ICD-10), risk score, RAF (Risk Adjustment Factor), submission period, submission type (initial, supplemental, deletion), RADV audit status, and payment impact amount. Supports risk adjustment revenue optimization and CMS compliance.'
}

Table insurance.coordination_of_benefits {
  coordination_of_benefits_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the coordination of benefits record. Primary key.']
  member_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.member_enrollment. Business justification: COB relationships are managed at the member enrollment level — each enrollment period may have different COB arrangements. Linking coordination_of_benefits to member_enrollment enables direct navigati']
  primary_coordination_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the health plan designated as primary coverage.']
  primary_coordination_member_mpi_record_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the member with multiple insurance coverages. Links to the patient/member master record.']
  primary_insurance_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to patient.insurance_coverage. Business justification: COB processing requires referencing the patient\'s specific primary insurance_coverage record to determine payer sequencing, crossover claim routing, and cost-sharing calculation. A healthcare billing ']
  primary_payer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance payer designated as primary in the coordination of benefits hierarchy.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the primary subscriber on the insurance policy.']
  superseded_coordination_of_benefits_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on coordination_of_benefits (superseded_coordination_of_benefits_id)']
  tertiary_payer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the insurance payer designated as tertiary in the coordination of benefits hierarchy, if applicable.']
  tertiary_plan_health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the health plan designated as tertiary coverage, if applicable.']
  auto_cob_update_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic updates to COB information are enabled through eligibility feeds or third-party vendors. True when automated updates are active.']
  cob_agreement_type varchar [note: 'The type of coordination agreement between payers. Standard COB allows combined benefits up to 100% of charges; non-duplication pays only what primary did not cover; carve-out excludes certain services; maintenance of benefits pays as if primary regardless of other coverage.']
  cob_determination_method varchar [note: 'The method or rule used to determine the order of benefit responsibility among multiple coverages. Common methods include birthday rule (parent with earlier birthday is primary for dependent children), gender rule (deprecated method using father as primary), NAIC COB rules, court order, separation agreement, or active/inactive employment status.']
  cob_effective_date date [note: 'The date when the coordination of benefits arrangement becomes effective and the payer order applies for claim adjudication.']
  cob_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, exceptions, or additional context regarding the coordination of benefits arrangement.']
  cob_priority_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence indicating the order of benefit responsibility. 1 = primary, 2 = secondary, 3 = tertiary. Used for claim adjudication sequencing.']
  cob_questionnaire_response_date date [note: 'The date when the member returned the completed COB questionnaire with updated coverage information.']
  cob_questionnaire_sent_date date [note: 'The date when a COB questionnaire was sent to the member to verify or update other insurance coverage information.']
  cob_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the coordination of benefits arrangement.']
  cob_termination_date date [note: 'The date when the coordination of benefits arrangement ends, typically due to loss of one coverage or change in member circumstances.']
  cob_verification_date date [note: 'The date when the coordination of benefits information was last verified with the member or through external sources.']
  cob_verification_source varchar [note: 'The source or method used to verify the coordination of benefits information, such as member attestation, employer report, eligibility file feed, claims data analysis, third-party COB vendor, or manual research.']
  court_order_date date [note: 'The date of the court order that specifies the coordination of benefits arrangement, if applicable.']
  court_order_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a court order specifies the coordination of benefits order, overriding standard COB rules. True when court order exists.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this coordination of benefits record was first created in the system.']
  crossover_claim_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this COB arrangement involves automatic crossover claims between Medicare and Medicaid or other secondary payers. True indicates crossover claims are processed automatically.']
  custody_arrangement varchar [note: 'The custody arrangement for dependent children when parents are separated or divorced, which may override standard birthday rule COB determination per court order or separation agreement.']
  dependent_relationship_code varchar [note: 'The relationship of the member to the subscriber, used in determining COB order under birthday rule or other dependent coverage rules.']
  last_cob_inquiry_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent COB inquiry or verification was performed with external sources or the member.']
  medicare_primary_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether Medicare is the primary payer in this coordination of benefits arrangement. True when Medicare pays first.']
  msp_type varchar [note: 'The type of Medicare Secondary Payer situation, if applicable. Identifies the reason Medicare is secondary: working aged (group health plan coverage), ESRD (end-stage renal disease coordination period), disability (large group health plan), no-fault insurance, workers compensation, or liability insurance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: working_aged|esrd|disability|no_fault|workers_comp|liability|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_cob_verification_due_date date [note: 'The date when the next periodic COB verification is scheduled to ensure accuracy of payer order information.']
  primary_coverage_type varchar [note: 'The type of insurance coverage designated as primary in the COB hierarchy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: group|individual|medicare|medicaid|tricare|va|workers_comp|auto_insurance|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which this coordination of benefits record originated (e.g., Epic Resolute, Cerner Revenue Cycle, eligibility vendor feed).']
  subscriber_birth_date date [note: 'Date of birth of the primary subscriber, used in birthday rule calculations for dependent COB determination.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last updated this coordination of benefits record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this coordination of benefits record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Master record managing coordination of benefits (COB) rules and active COB relationships for members with multiple insurance coverages. Captures member ID, primary payer ID, primary plan ID, secondary payer ID, secondary plan ID, tertiary payer information, COB determination method (birthday rule, gender rule, NAIC COB rules), COB effective date, COB verification date, COB verification source, and crossover claim indicator (Medicare/Medicaid crossover). SSOT for COB order determination referenced during claim adjudication to prevent duplicate payment and ensure correct primary/secondary billing sequencing.'
}

Table insurance.group_plan_offering {
  group_plan_offering_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the group_plan_offering association']
  employer_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employer group that is electing this health plan offering.']
  health_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the health plan product being offered to the employer group.']
  benefit_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for which this group plan offering is in effect. Enables year-over-year historical tracking of which plans a group offered in each benefit year.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this specific group plan offering becomes active — i.e., when the employer group\'s election of this health plan takes effect. Belongs to the offering, not to the plan or group alone, as different groups activate the same plan on different dates.']
  employer_contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the employee-only premium that the employer pays for this specific plan offering. An employer may contribute 80% toward the HMO but only 70% toward the PPO — this value is plan-specific and belongs to the offering, not to the employer group record.']
  enrollment_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the open enrollment window during which employees of this employer group may elect this specific health plan. May differ from the plan\'s general open enrollment dates.']
  enrollment_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the open enrollment window during which employees of this employer group may elect this specific health plan. May differ from the plan\'s general open enrollment dates.']
  minimum_participation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of eligible employees that must enroll in this specific health plan for the group offering to remain active. This threshold is negotiated per plan per group and belongs to the offering, not to either entity alone.']
  offering_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this group plan offering. Tracks whether the offering is currently active, pending activation, terminated, or suspended. Belongs to the offering record, not to the plan or group.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date on which this group plan offering ends. Enables historical tracking of which plans were offered in prior benefit years. Belongs to the offering, not to the plan or group alone.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between health_plan and employer_group. It captures the specific terms under which a payer offers a health plan product to an employer group, including the employer\'s contribution rate, enrollment windows, and participation requirements for that plan. Each record links one health_plan to one employer_group and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this group-specific plan offering — not on the plan itself (which is a master template) nor on the employer group (which may offer multiple plans).. Existence Justification: In group health insurance operations, a payer offers multiple health plan products (HMO, PPO, HDHP, etc.) to an employer group, and the same plan product is sold to many employer groups — this is a genuine operational M:N. The relationship is actively managed as a \'Group Plan Offering\' or \'Employer Plan Election\': benefits administrators create, modify, and terminate these offerings each renewal cycle, tracking contribution rates, enrollment windows, and participation thresholds that belong to neither the plan nor the group alone. This is a well-recognized, named business concept in healthcare insurance operations.'
}
